verdicts.rationale: This is not a knowledge skill but the bootstrap injector for the superpowers plugin: a SessionStart hook pastes its full text into every session to mandate skill invocation before any response, routing to superpowers-specific skills (brainstorming, systematic-debugging) David does not use. It teaches nothing the library lacks — domain-reference already documents how triggering works, and Claude Code natively keeps every skill's description in context. Worse, it actively contradicts David's architecture: its non-negotiable 1%-threshold rule is the exact ceremony-on-trivia failure that architecture-contract Decision 4 makes a first-class invariant against, and its always-on description is the pattern Decision 1 explicitly fences off; the library instead pursues trigger reliability through measured description quality (governance-adoption-campaign). Its only residual value is as a design reference for architecture-contract's unexplored weak-point-3 hook-hardening path — worth a bookmark in that discussion, not an install. Standalone, without replicating the whole superpowers hook architecture, it is dead weight on Claude Code and unusable on claude.ai.

verdicts.name: verification-before-completion

inv.name: verification-before-completion
inv.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
inv.purpose: Blocks false 'done/fixed/passing' claims by requiring a fresh verification command, in the current message, before any success statement or expression of satisfaction. Frames unverified claims as dishonesty, not efficiency.
inv.key_content: Iron Law: 'NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE'. 5-step Gate Function (IDENTIFY the proving command → RUN fresh and complete → READ full output/exit code → VERIFY it confirms the claim → only then claim), with 'skip any step = lying, not verifying'. Claim-to-evidence table: tests pass requires 0-failure output (not a previous run); bug fixed requires re-testing the original symptom; regression test requires a full red-green cycle — write, pass, REVERT THE FIX, watch it fail, restore, pass again; agent completed requires checking the VCS diff, never trusting the agent's own success report. Red flags include 'should/probably/seems to' and premature satisfaction words ('Great!', 'Perfect!'). Rationalization table ('I'm confident' → 'Confidence ≠ evidence'; 'Agent said success' → 'Verify independently'). Applies to paraphrases and implications, not just exact phrases. Motivation section citing 24 real failure memories and the trust-breaking 'I don't believe you' moment.
inv.surfaces: Both as a principle, but operationally Claude Code — the evidence it demands comes from running test/build commands and git diffs.
inv.dependencies: Ability to execute the project's verification commands; referenced by systematic-debugging and the SDD review loop; pairs with TDD's red-green discipline.
inv.quality: Substantive discipline enforcement. The revert-to-verify regression pattern and never-trust-subagent-reports rule are concrete and beyond default behavior; the rest overlaps increasingly with newer models' verification habits but the explicit gate wording still measurably tightens behavior. Terse, no padding.

verdicts.overlaps_with: adversarial-verify (core, near-total: same trigger moment, same evidence-before-claims law, same honest-reporting contract); Claude Code built-in `verify` skill (end-to-end exercise of changes before commit); base-model verification habits (hedge-word avoidance, running tests before claiming pass)
verdicts.surfaces: Both in principle; operationally Claude Code (its evidence is test/build command output and git diffs), where the overlap with the built-in `verify` skill is also strongest.
verdicts.license: MIT (Copyright 2025 Jesse Vincent, obra/superpowers)
verdicts.rationale: The candidate's entire trigger territory is already owned by adversarial-verify, whose description fires at the identical moment ("whenever you are about to write 'done', 'fixed', or 'verified'") and whose body already enforces the candidate's core: the evidence hierarchy puts fresh-context behavioral checks on top and dismisses inspection-only impressions as non-evidence (the Iron Law), the Behavioral-check section mandates running the proving command (the Gate Function), and the delivery contract requires evidence beside each PASS/FAIL with no oversell (claim-with-evidence). On Claude Code the built-in `verify` skill separately covers exercising changes end-to-end before commit, and the rationalization/red-flag tables largely restate discipline current base models exhibit — the same standard that got live-state-truth retired. Only two concrete patterns are net-new: the regression-test red-green revert cycle (write, pass, revert the fix, watch it fail, restore, pass) and never trusting a subagent's success report without checking the VCS diff — roughly two table rows of content, best proposed as an amendment to adversarial-verify's attack table through research-methodology's A/B process. Installing the candidate as its own skill would put two governors in competition at the exact same trigger moment, which the strict-tiered footprint and single-owner trigger design exist to prevent.

verdicts.name: writing-plans

inv.name: writing-plans
inv.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
inv.purpose: Turns an approved spec into an implementation plan written for 'an engineer with zero context for our codebase and questionable taste': every task has exact file paths, complete code, exact commands with expected output, and 2-5 minute TDD steps. The plan is the contract that makes subagent execution reliable.
inv.key_content: Audience framing (skilled developer, zero domain/toolset context, poor test-design instincts). Save location docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature>.md. Mandatory plan header embedding a machine-directive ('REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development...') plus Goal/Architecture/Tech Stack and a Global Constraints section with spec values copied VERBATIM (this becomes every SDD reviewer's attention lens). Task template with Files (Create/Modify with line ranges/Test) and an Interfaces block (Consumes/Produces with exact signatures — how implementers who see only their own task learn neighboring names/types). 5-step checkbox TDD cadence per task (write failing test → run to see fail with expected message → minimal implementation → run to pass → commit). 'No Placeholders' list declaring TBD/'add appropriate error handling'/'similar to Task N' to be plan FAILURES. Task right-sizing rule: smallest unit carrying its own test cycle and worth a fresh reviewer's gate. 3-point self-review (spec coverage, placeholder scan, type consistency across tasks — clearLayers() vs clearFullLayers() example). Scripted execution handoff offering SDD vs executing-plans.
inv.surfaces: Claude Code leaning — the artifact is markdown (writable anywhere) but the format hard-assumes git commits, test runners, and Superpowers executors.
inv.dependencies: An approved spec from brainstorming (upstream); consumed by subagent-driven-development / executing-plans; plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md companion; SDD's task-brief script parses the plan's task structure.
inv.quality: Substantive. The Interfaces consumed/produced block, verbatim Global Constraints, and the placeholders-are-failures rule are the specific mechanisms that make fresh-context subagent execution work, and they are not default model behavior. The bite-sized 5-step cadence can feel mechanical for expert humans but is calibrated for its actual audience: cheap-model subagents.

verdicts.overlaps_with: plan-gate (partial: plan-before-acting discipline, phased decomposition); Claude Code plan mode + Workflow orchestration/subagents (built-in planning and delegated execution, but not the zero-context executable plan artifact)
verdicts.surfaces: Claude Code primarily — the artifact format hard-assumes git commits, test runners, and subagent execution; on claude.ai it can only draft the markdown, so it's marginal there
verdicts.license: MIT (Copyright 2025 Jesse Vincent)
verdicts.rationale: Verified against the actual SKILL.md: the net-new capability is the zero-context executable plan DOCUMENT — Interfaces Consumes/Produces blocks with exact signatures so each task's fresh-context implementer learns neighboring names/types, Global Constraints copied verbatim from the spec, complete code and exact commands with expected output in every step, and the no-placeholders-are-plan-failures rule with a type-consistency self-review across tasks. plan-gate covers the upstream discipline (falsifiable goal, assumptions, pre-committed criteria, phased plan) but produces no implementation artifact; Claude Code's plan mode and Workflow orchestration plan and delegate but do not enforce this handoff-reliable document format, and none of it is default model behavior. It is narrower than MUST ADD: Code-only in practice, fires only at the spec-to-plan moment in delegated/multi-task implementation work, and requires an adaptation pass to strip the hard-coded superpowers pipeline directives (mandatory subagent-driven-development header, docs/superpowers/plans/ path, worktree sub-skill reference) and re-point the execution handoff at Claude Code's native subagent machinery. Place on-demand: invoke when a spec is in hand and implementation will be delegated or long-running; always-on per-session cost is not justified since plan-gate already owns the always-on planning trigger. The companion plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md is a worthwhile bundled extra for a review subagent pass.

verify.reasoning: Read the candidate (scratchpad .../writing-plans/SKILL.md + plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) and the core skills plan-gate/SKILL.md, architecture-contract/SKILL.md, skill-authoring/SKILL.md. (1) REDUNDANCY: the analyst's net-new claim is only half true. Genuinely absent from plan-gate, plan mode, and Workflow/subagents: the Interfaces Consumes/Produces blocks (SKILL.md:89-93), the no-placeholders failure list (:129-137), exact-command-with-expected-output, and the type-consistency self-review (:152) — plan-gate (read in full) produces goal/assumptions/criteria/phases, never an implementation artifact. But the candidate's central premise — a zero-context implementer who 'sees only their own task' — is a superpowers-pipeline fact, not an environmental one: in Claude Code the parent composes each subagent's prompt and can pass any context, so much of the artifact's value (complete blind-written code in every step) is compensating for a constraint this library does not have; blind plan-time code for strong models with codebase access is contestable methodology, exactly the kind of thing measurement could retire under the live-state-truth standard. Roughly a third of the body is non-portable plumbing: mandatory superpowers:subagent-driven-development header (:61), docs/superpowers/plans/ path (:18), worktree sub-skill (:16), Execution Handoff naming two superpowers skills (:158-174). (2) ARCHITECTURE: as-is it breaks three things. Decision 2 (no repo-specific facts in procedure) — superpowers paths and sub-skill names are procedure text, not lineage. Decision 3 (description carries WHEN + quoted phrases + NOT clause) — its description, 'Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code,' has no quoted phrasings, no NOT clause, and is nearly plan-gate's exact firing condition, guaranteeing double-loads at the start of every spec-driven task. Decision 4 / invariant 5 (anti-ceremony is first-class) — the body has NO proportionality valve at all: mandatory announcement line (:14), a rigid five-step 2-5-minute TDD cycle per task regardless of size, checkbox tracking — every governor in this library carries a triage rule; this one has none. All fixable, but the required rewrite plus research-methodology gating is substantial, and adaptation burden is tier-relevant. (3) SUBSTANCE: partially passes. The placeholder-ban list and Interfaces contract are concrete format-changing rules, not be-careful prose — strong models genuinely do emit 'add appropriate error handling' in plans. But other credited content (exact file paths, map file structure first, follow existing patterns, self-review against spec) is plausibly baseline Fable-5/Opus behavior by the same standard that retired live-state-truth (architecture-contract Decision 7: zero delta across eight cells), and nothing here is measured (weak-point 1). Net: the surviving delta is a narrower core than the analyst credited, the trigger placement collides with the library's only always-on planning governor, and value is entirely unmeasured with a high adaptation cost. That profile is 'worth keeping in the drawer for delegated multi-task implementation work after a rewrite' — NICE TO HAVE, not HIGHLY RECOMMEND. Torn between the two, the instruction says choose the lower; the evidence independently points there anyway.

verdicts.name: writing-skills

inv.name: writing-skills
inv.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
inv.purpose: The meta-skill: treats skill authoring as TDD applied to process documentation — run pressure scenarios on agents WITHOUT the skill (RED), write the skill against their verbatim rationalizations (GREEN), then close loopholes (REFACTOR). Also the house style guide for frontmatter, naming, token budgets, and description writing.
inv.key_content: Iron Law: 'NO SKILL WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST' — applies to edits too. TDD-to-skill mapping table (pressure scenario = test case; baseline violation = red). The empirically-grounded SDO rule that a description must state ONLY triggering conditions, never summarize the workflow — with the documented failure: a description saying 'code review between tasks' caused an agent to do ONE review instead of the body's two-stage review; removing the summary fixed it. Frontmatter constraints (1024-char max, third person, 'Use when...', keyword coverage with error strings/symptoms/synonyms; verb-first gerund names like condition-based-waiting). Token-efficiency budgets (<150 words for always-loaded workflows, <500 otherwise) and the ban on @-links (force-load burns 200k context). 'Match the Form to the Failure' table with A/B evidence: prohibitions bulletproof discipline violations but BACKFIRE on output-shaping problems (the prohibition arm produced more unwanted content than no guidance at all — use positive recipes); nuance clauses ('unless it matters') measurably degrade winning recipes; exemption clauses don't scope. Micro-testing protocol: fresh-context single samples, mandatory no-guidance control, 5+ reps per variant, manually read every flagged match, variance-as-metric. Bulletproofing toolkit: close every loophole explicitly, 'letter = spirit' preamble, rationalization tables built from real transcripts, red-flag lists; persuasion-principles.md grounds it in Cialdini/Meincke research. Per-type test strategies for discipline/technique/pattern/reference skills. Full RED-GREEN-REFACTOR deployment checklist; anti-patterns (narrative examples, multi-language dilution, code in flowcharts).
inv.surfaces: Claude Code only for the full methodology (subagent pressure-testing, wc, git deployment); the authoring principles themselves transfer to any skill library including claude.ai skills.
inv.dependencies: Requires understanding test-driven-development (declared REQUIRED BACKGROUND); subagent dispatch for testing; companions testing-skills-with-subagents.md (384 lines), persuasion-principles.md, anthropic-best-practices.md (1150 lines, Anthropic's official guidance included for contrast), graphviz-conventions.dot, render-graphs.js.
inv.quality: The most intellectually valuable skill in the repo — the description-summary trap, prohibition-backfire finding, and micro-testing protocol are original, evidence-backed contributions not available in Anthropic's own docs (which the repo explicitly diverges from, per CLAUDE.md). Long, but almost entirely signal. Directly reusable for building any personal skill library.

verdicts.overlaps_with: skill-authoring (house style, description contract, authoring checklist — with a conflicting description standard), research-methodology (wording A/B discipline; candidate adds no-guidance control, variance-as-metric, manual-read rule), governance-adoption-campaign (the pressure-scenario methodology this campaign needs but doesn't yet have); minor: diagnostics-and-tooling (token/wc checks), domain-reference (what-is-a-skill)
verdicts.surfaces: Claude Code for the full methodology (subagent pressure-testing, wc, render-graphs.js); the authoring/testing principles transfer to claude.ai skill work as read-along reference
verdicts.license: MIT (Copyright 2025 Jesse Vincent)
verdicts.rationale: Verified against the actual files: the net-new payload is real and evidence-backed — the RED-baseline discipline with mandatory no-guidance control (if agents don't fail without the skill, don't write it — the exact doctrine that retired live-state-truth, turned into a pre-authoring gate), the Match-the-Form-to-the-Failure table with A/B evidence that prohibitions backfire on output-shaping problems and nuance clauses degrade winning recipes, the SDO description-summary trap, and the bulletproofing toolkit (pressure scenarios with combined pressures, rationalization tables from verbatim transcripts, red-flag lists) that governance-adoption-campaign needs and nothing in the core library provides. But it is meta-domain (fires only when authoring/testing skills), and it partially overlaps skill-authoring and research-methodology while prescribing a conflicting house standard (≤500-char no-NOT-clause descriptions vs David's three-job ~1000-char contract), so installing it live would create a direct trigger collision with skill-authoring — a routing bug by the library's own architecture-contract. Correct integration: keep it on-demand as the methodology/evidence reference (standout files: SKILL.md's Match-the-Form and micro-testing sections, testing-skills-with-subagents.md, persuasion-principles.md) and mine its findings into skill-authoring and research-methodology via the library's own gated research-methodology process. HIGHLY RECOMMEND rather than MUST ADD because leverage is narrow-domain and overlap is substantial; rather than NICE TO HAVE because skill authoring IS this library's core activity and the candidate directly addresses its hardest live problem (proving governors trigger and change behavior).

verify.reasoning: REDUNDANCY (fails the analyst's headline claim). The tier's central pillar — that the RED-baseline/no-guidance-control doctrine ("if agents don't fail without the skill, don't write it") is net-new payload the library lacks — is false. That doctrine is already codified AND executed in the core library at a higher evidentiary standard than the candidate prescribes: governance-adoption-campaign/SKILL.md Phase 2 contains the explicit branch rule "If without-library runs ALSO show the behavior: the base model already does it... consider whether the governor earns its context cost", and the library ran exactly that control-arm protocol (40 paired A/B runs, uncued discriminating tests, cross-model Sonnet runs, blind grading, sha256-verified install toggling, pre-registration committed before runs) — producing architecture-contract Decision 7's retirements. The analyst cites live-state-truth's retirement as proof the candidate's doctrine works, but that retirement was achieved BY the library's own existing methodology, proving the doctrine is already operational, not missing. The candidate's version (5+ rep micro-tests, single-shot subagents) is a weaker instrument than what the library already ran. Further redundancy: anthropic-best-practices.md is 1150 lines of official Anthropic docs (web-fetchable, largely base-model-known); the SDO/description sections prescribe a standard (≤500 chars, "Use when...", NEVER state what the skill does, no NOT clause, gerund verb-first names) that directly contradicts skill-authoring's verified three-job ≤1000-char contract (WHEN + quoted phrasings + NOT clause, eval-tested 2026-07-11) — conflicting guidance is negative value under Decision 2's "duplication is drift waiting to happen". What is genuinely net-new is narrower than claimed: the Match-the-Form-to-the-Failure table (prohibition-vs-recipe A/B evidence, nuance-clause degradation), the bulletproofing toolkit (combined-pressure scenarios, verbatim rationalization tables, red-flag lists, meta-testing), variance-as-metric and manual-read-every-match refinements, and persuasion-principles' Meincke et al. citation. Real, but idea-mine material, not missing methodology. TIMELINESS of the need-claim also fails: the analyst says governance-adoption-campaign "needs but doesn't yet have" this methodology, but the campaign's FINAL ACCEPTANCE passed 2026-07-11 (SHIPPED / regression-clean) and claude.ai acceptance passed 2026-07-12; the remaining open problems — scope-fence's trigger ceiling (diagnosed as unfireable-by-wording, addressed by the shipped PreToolUse hook) and other-surface rollout — are not addressed by pressure scenarios or form-matching at all. ARCHITECTURE: installed live, the candidate's description ("Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work") collides head-on with skill-authoring and research-methodology — a routing bug under Decision 3 and the boundary-map invariant; the analyst correctly conceded this by proposing on-demand placement, but an artifact that cannot be installed and whose usable content must be filtered through the house contract and mined via gated research-methodology experiments is reference material, not a HIGHLY RECOMMEND addition. SUBSTANCE: passes — the Match-the-Form table and testing-skills-with-subagents.md are concrete, evidence-backed, and would change authoring behavior; this is not retire-on-measurement prose (though its Iron Law absolutism, "edit without test? delete it", contradicts the library's own successful author-then-measure practice, and its token-efficiency/SDO bulk duplicates official docs). Net: substance check passes, but the redundancy check refutes the tier's primary rationale and the architecture check confines the candidate to a mine-from reference. Applying the tie-goes-lower rule: NICE TO HAVE — keep on-demand as an idea source (Match-the-Form table, rationalization-table toolkit, pressure-scenario formats, SDO-trap hypothesis) to seed research-methodology experiments; do not install.

verdicts.name: fable-method

inv.name: fable-method
inv.description: A step-by-step problem-solving loop (classify the ask, define done, gather evidence, decide, act surgically, verify by observation, report outcome-first). Use when the user says "/fable-method", "use the fable method", or "approach this like Fable", or proactively when starting any multi-step task that no task-specific skill covers. Subcommands - plan (stop after the plan), audit (grade finished work against the loop), report (rewrite an answer outcome-first).
inv.purpose: The core skill: a 7-step work loop (Step 0 classify through Step 6 report) intended to let a mid-tier model produce frontier-quality agentic work by following structure literally. It governs the entire lifecycle of any non-trivial task — classification, definition of done, evidence gathering, decision, surgical editing, observed verification, and outcome-first reporting — with explicit tie-breaks, numeric budgets, and hard stop bounds at every branch. Three subcommand modes (plan / audit / report) reuse slices of the loop.
inv.key_content: Distinctive, concrete machinery: (1) a quantified triviality gate (one file, <~10 changed lines, no new behavior, no searching — ALL must hold to skip the loop); (2) Step 0 classification table (question/task/plan-first) with ordered tie-breaks — plan-first beats task, a mixed ask is a task whose report must answer the question, genuine uncertainty defaults to plan-first — plus an ambiguous-scope test (can you imagine two materially different deliverables?) and the rule to ask exactly ONE pointed question stating your recommended interpretation, never asking what evidence can settle; (3) the INTENT gate: before any behavior-changing edit write the line 'INTENT: code does <X>; the failing check/task expects <Y>; the spec says <Z>', which requires actually opening the spec and must appear VERBATIM in the final report — with the authority order: explicit user statement > spec > tests > current code behavior, and the crucial clarification that task framing like 'make the tests pass' is NOT a statement of intended behavior; (4) mechanical evidence budget: one lookup round + one follow-up, a third needs a stated reason, two fruitless consecutive lookups = stop; orient-first (glob before reading); parallelize only independent expensive lookups, chain small adaptive local reads; (5) reversibility test: irreversible = another person/system can observe it before you could undo it; (6) checklist trigger: 3+ heterogeneous steps or >~5 similar items; (7) failed-edit recovery ladder: re-read + retry once, then widen, full rewrite last and disclosed, never retry a failed call verbatim; (8) two-halves verification (done criterion observed + surrounding system healthy) with a hard bound of 3 failed fix-verify cycles then mandatory hand-back with output and hypothesis; (9) Step 6: no step numbers/scaffolding in the report, delete scratch artifacts and disclose the cleanup, hostile-reviewer reread before sending, follow-ups only if they emerged from the work. Reference files: failure-modes.md (14 failure modes mapped symptom→preventing step, e.g. #14 verification theater), examples.md (one worked example per ask shape), flowcharts.md (7 Mermaid decision charts billed as executable pseudocode, corrected against observed Fable traces in eval round 10), and 7 domain adapters (marketing, research, data-analysis, business-ops, finance, legal-compliance, design-ux) each defining evidence, authority order, verification meaning, a fraud table for fable-judge, and a BINDING minimum evidence set; medical/clinical deliberately has no adapter.
inv.surfaces: Both. The core loop is pure procedural discipline with no hard tool requirement — it hedges appropriately ('a todo tool if the harness has one', 'context7 if available'), and references/ are bundled inside the skill directory so a .skill package works on claude.ai. Full value (running builds/tests for Step 5) leans Claude Code, but nothing breaks elsewhere.
inv.dependencies: None hard. Optional: its own references/ subtree (failure-modes.md, examples.md, flowcharts.md, domains/*, loaded lazily); context7 MCP for doc lookups if present. It is a dependency OF the other two skills: fable-loop mandates reading it first and fable-judge borrows its failure-modes catalogue and domain fraud tables.
inv.quality: Substantive — the strongest of the three and clearly beyond be-careful prose: nearly every rule carries a number, an ordered tie-break, or a forced artifact, and the eval log documents which rules moved measured behavior (the INTENT gate took Haiku from 0/4 to 4/4 on the spec-vs-test trap). Honest caveat from the repo's own data: for Sonnet-class and above on small attended tasks the loop measurably adds nothing (rounds 1, 6, 7 nulls), so for a strong model much of it is ceremony; its proven value concentrates on weak executors and authority-conflict traps. High overlap in territory with plan-gate (Steps 0-1, plan-first stop), scope-fence (smallest correct change, blast radius), and adversarial-verify (Step 5-6 hostile reread) but the distilled artifacts — INTENT line, authority order, numeric bounds, recovery ladder, triviality thresholds — are more mechanical than the equivalents in most governance libraries. Fresh (updated 2026-07-09); one wart: it references the 'GSD workflow' (via fable-loop) which is never defined anywhere in the repo.

verdicts.overlaps_with: plan-gate (Steps 0-3: triviality triage, falsifiable done, pre-committed verification, plan-first stop), adversarial-verify (Steps 5-6: observed two-halves verification, hostile-reviewer reread, outcome-first honest caveats, audit/report subcommands), scope-fence (Step 4: smallest correct change, no drive-by refactors), retired live-state-truth doctrine (Step 2: primary sources over memory), plus Claude Code built-ins plan mode and /verify
verdicts.surfaces: Both — references/ are bundled so a .skill upload works on claude.ai; full Step-5 value (running builds/tests) leans Claude Code, where it also installs as a plugin
verdicts.license: MIT
verdicts.rationale: Roughly 80% of the loop is the core library restated as a monolith: Steps 0-3 are plan-gate's triage/goal/criteria/plan, Step 4 is scope-fence's smallest-correct-change, Steps 5-6 are adversarial-verify's observed verification and honest delivery, and Step 2's primary-sources rule is the doctrine David already retired (live-state-truth) on measured base-model evidence — the repo's own eval log confirms this, showing nulls for Sonnet-class models on ordinary attended tasks (rounds 1, 6, 7), so always-on installation would double-govern every task with a competing triviality gate and duplicated ceremony. What IS net-new and mechanical: the INTENT gate (forced verbatim artifact 'code does X / check expects Y / spec says Z' plus the authority order user > spec > tests > code and the rule that "make the tests pass" is not a statement of intent), the numeric hard bounds (3 failed fix-verify cycles then hand back, 2 fruitless lookups then stop, failed-edit recovery ladder), and the 7 domain adapters with binding minimum evidence sets — none of which exist in plan-gate, adversarial-verify, or the built-ins, and the INTENT gate demonstrably improved Sonnet itself on the authority-conflict trap (round 3: from siding with a wrong test to ideal action 8/8). Right disposition: keep as an on-demand .skill/explicit invocation for delegated, unattended, or weak-executor (Haiku subagent) runs where its proven value concentrates, and separately harvest the INTENT gate and retry bounds into plan-gate/adversarial-verify through the research-methodology A/B process rather than importing the whole loop. Standout sub-parts within this skill: the INTENT gate, references/failure-modes.md (a solid 14-row audit checklist for agent transcripts, distinct from debugging-playbook's skill-failure scope), and the domain adapters' fraud tables.

verdicts.name: fable-loop

inv.name: fable-loop
inv.description: End-to-end orchestrated workflow that runs a task the way Fable ran sessions - parallel evidence subagents, one committed plan, surgical execution with an intent gate, adversarial verification agents, honest outcome-first report. Use for non-trivial multi-step tasks when the user says "/fable-loop", "run the fable loop", or "do this the way Fable would". For the rules alone without orchestration, use fable-method; …[truncated: routes large multi-phase projects to the GSD workflow]
inv.purpose: The orchestration layer over fable-method: the method says WHAT to check, this says WHO does the work — what stays in the main thread versus what fans out to subagents, across four stages (PLAN, EXECUTE, VERIFY, AUDIT/REPORT). It converts the method's rules into a subagent topology with an explicit decision gate for approval and adversarial verifier agents before delivery.
inv.key_content: Stage 1 PLAN: apply method Steps 0-3, then evidence fan-out as parallel subagents in ONE message (an Explore agent per distinct codebase area, a research agent for library/fact questions; subagents return distilled cited findings, never raw dumps; budget = one batch + one follow-up); produce a fixed-shape plan artifact (classification, done+verification, cited evidence, ONE recommendation with alternatives dismissed in a line each, risks/assumptions, execution checklist); decision gate — task-shaped and reversible proceeds WITHOUT asking, plan-first shape presents the plan and STOPS. Stage 2 EXECUTE: deciding and editing stay in the main thread, only searching and verifying fan out; independent mechanical items may fan out in one message WITH worktree isolation if they could touch the same files; a mid-execution surprise re-routes per method rule 7, never force the plan through a surprise. Stage 3 VERIFY: run the named verification yourself first (both halves), then for consequential changes spawn 1-3 parallel ATTACKER subagents each prompted to REFUTE from a distinct lens (prove the diff wrong/incomplete; exercise the changed behavior at runtime and find a breaking input; check the claim against spec/docs for a contradiction) — 'distinct lenses beat identical reviewers'; surviving findings go back to Stage 2 as new work; same 3-cycle hard bound. Stage 4: self-audit per fable-method audit mode (followed/skipped/faked per step), then report with no stage names — the INTENT line is the only permitted method artifact. Model economy section: main thread on the strongest model, evidence/attacker subagents cheap-model-friendly, attackers get higher effort than gatherers.
inv.surfaces: Claude Code only. The entire skill is about spawning parallel subagents in one message, Explore/research agent types, worktree isolation, and a todo tool — none of which exist on claude.ai. It also assumes fable-method is co-installed at a filesystem path.
inv.dependencies: Hard dependency on fable-method (must read its SKILL.md first; gives both plugin-relative and ~/.claude/skills lookup paths). Requires a subagent-capable harness (Task/Explore agents), git worktrees for isolated parallel edits, ideally a todo tool. References the external 'GSD workflow' for multi-phase projects without defining or shipping it.
inv.quality: Lean (49 lines) and genuinely orchestration-specific rather than a restatement of the method — the main-thread-vs-fan-out division, the one-message batching rule, and the three named attacker lenses are concrete and copyable. However it is the least evidence-backed skill in the repo BY THE REPO'S OWN LOG: its only eval outing (round 7) was a clean null — 12/12 runs scored 8/8 in ALL conditions including bare Sonnet — so the repo can only claim 'the orchestration adds no noise', not that it adds value; its value case (large multi-file tasks, weak executors, unattended runs) is explicitly untested. The dangling GSD reference is an assumption imported from the author's environment and mild staleness risk.

verdicts.overlaps_with: plan-gate (Stage 1: classify, define done with named verification, assumptions, one-recommendation plan artifact, decision gate = plan-gate's triage + gate + phased plan); adversarial-verify (Stage 3 refutation with distinct attack lenses + Stage 4 outcome-first honest report = adversarial-verify's refutation table and delivery contract); Claude Code built-ins: plan mode, subagents/Agent tool + Workflow orchestration (parallel fan-out, worktrees), verify, code-review. Also hard-depends on fable-method (sibling candidate, not in core library).
verdicts.surfaces: Claude Code only — the entire body is subagent fan-out, Explore/research agents, worktree isolation, and a todo tool, none of which exist on claude.ai, so half the owner's equally-weighted surfaces get zero value.
verdicts.license: MIT (Copyright 2026 Sahir619)
verdicts.rationale: The four stages restate what David already runs: Stage 1 is plan-gate (goal/done/assumptions/plan artifact/decision gate), Stages 3-4 are adversarial-verify (refutation attacks, criteria grading, honest outcome-first delivery), and the orchestration substrate (parallel subagents in one message, worktrees, plan mode) is Claude Code built-in capability the harness already prompts for. The genuinely net-new sliver is thin: the explicit fan-out topology rules (one-batch evidence budget, main-thread-decides/subagents-gather) and the three named parallel attacker lenses — a few lines that could be folded into adversarial-verify if ever proven to help. By the repo's own RESULTS.md (Round 7, verified), the loop's only eval was a clean null: 12/12 runs scored 8/8 in all conditions including bare Sonnet, so the repo can only claim the orchestration "adds no noise," not value; adopting a skill on a measured null would invert the exact evidence standard under which this library retired live-state-truth. It also cannot install standalone — it hard-requires fable-method (which would wholesale duplicate plan-gate/adversarial-verify/scope-fence) and dangles a reference to an unshipped GSD workflow. If the attacker-lens prompts ever prove out on large multi-file tasks, port those three sentences into adversarial-verify rather than adding this skill.

verdicts.name: fable-judge

inv.name: fable-judge
inv.description: Adversarial verification of finished work. Treats any "done" as a set of claims, then re-runs the claimed verifications, diffs what actually changed, detects weakened tests and false completion claims, and delivers an evidence-based verdict (VERIFIED / VERIFIED WITH CAVEATS / REFUTED). Use after any agent or model claims work is complete - "/fable-judge", "judge this work", "verify what it did", "did that actually work?". …[truncated: also runs the trap suite via "/fable-judge suite <target>"]
inv.purpose: An adversarial verifier for finished work — its fixed stance is that a completion report is a set of claims, not evidence, and nothing is believed that was not observed. It converts any 'done' into rows to prove or refute via diffing and re-execution, hunts a taxonomy of agent frauds, and issues a three-valued verdict. A second mode turns it into an eval runner for the repo's own trap suite against any skill/model/prompt.
inv.key_content: Five-step procedure: (1) collect claims (done what / verified what / left-untouched what) as rows; (2) establish ground truth via git diff + git status, or a directory diff against a pristine reference — 'the diff is ground truth; the report is not' — compared against the ask's blast radius; (3) re-run EVERY claimed verification yourself, 'do not read code and nod'; anything un-rerunnable is labeled UNVERIFIABLE, never assumed true; (4) hunt the five classic frauds IN ORDER OF REAL-WORLD FREQUENCY: weakened checks (diff test files specifically — loosened/deleted assertions, expected values changed to match new behavior, skips, widened tolerances, mocks replacing real calls; 'a changed test is guilty until its justification traces to a spec'), false completion, scope creep, spec betrayal (with the user > spec > tests > code authority order), and debris (scratch files, debug prints, orphaned imports); non-code work is judged against its domain adapter's fraud table (e.g. copy checked line-by-line against brand.md, figures re-fetched, arithmetic recomputed); (5) verdict — VERIFIED / VERIFIED WITH CAVEATS / REFUTED — with a mandated format (verdict is the FIRST line, then claims table, frauds, recommended action) and a calibration rule: never soften a refutation to be polite, never inflate a caveat into a refutation to look rigorous. Standing rules: judging changes NOTHING (read and run only; fixes only if asked afterward); it is a gate, not a second implementation — minutes, not hours. Suite mode: runs each eval/scenarios/ fixture in a scratch copy via an executor subagent under the target configuration, then judges by diff and execution against GROUND-TRUTH, never by the executor's report; explicitly states one seed per scenario is a smoke test, not a benchmark.
inv.surfaces: Claude Code primarily: the default mode's spine is git diff and re-executing tests/builds (Bash), and suite mode additionally needs the repo's eval/ directory plus executor subagents. The domain-adapter fraud-hunting for documents/copy could run on claude.ai in principle, but the skill's core procedure is execution-based.
inv.dependencies: git and Bash for diffing and re-running verifications. Cross-skill: fable-method's references/failure-modes.md (used as the checklist on large work) and references/domains/ fraud tables (for non-code work) — so it degrades if installed without fable-method. Suite mode: the repo's eval/ directory (bundled in plugin installs at ../../eval/ relative to the SKILL.md; standalone installs need a separate clone of github.com/Sahir619/fable-method) plus workflow.js task/ground-truth definitions and subagents.
inv.quality: Substantive and the best-evidenced of the three: round 8 shows a real measured transfer (Haiku assessors went from 3.5/5 frauds caught with no code execution to 5/5 with max report quality; Sonnet was already at ceiling bare, honestly reported). The frequency-ordered fraud taxonomy, the guilty-until-spec-justified rule for changed tests, the UNVERIFIABLE label, and the both-directions calibration rule are concrete techniques a base model does not reliably apply unprompted. This is also the skill with the highest collision risk against adversarial-verify in the owner's library — same stance, same trigger territory ('did that actually work?') — so co-installing both invites double-firing; the distinctive deltas worth merging rather than duplicating are the verdict triad, diff-against-pristine-copy for non-git work, the fraud frequency ordering, the domain fraud tables, and suite mode with its shipped trap fixtures.

verdicts.overlaps_with: adversarial-verify (same refutation stance and trigger territory: "did that actually work?" / "verify it"); partially Claude Code built-ins verify (end-to-end exercise of changes) and code-review (diff bug hunting)
verdicts.surfaces: Claude Code primarily — the spine is git diff/git status plus Bash re-execution of tests/builds, and suite mode needs the repo's eval/ fixtures and executor subagents. Only the domain-adapter fraud-table judging of documents/copy transfers to claude.ai, and there it degrades without fable-method's references/domains/ files.
verdicts.license: MIT (Copyright (c) 2026 Sahir619)
verdicts.rationale: Verified against /home/user/claude-core-skills/.claude/skills/adversarial-verify/SKILL.md: the two share stance and trigger phrases, so co-installing both always-on invites double-firing — but fable-judge covers a case adversarial-verify does not: auditing ANOTHER agent's completion report by treating it as claims-to-reprove, re-running every claimed verification, and hunting a frequency-ordered fraud taxonomy (weakened tests with the guilty-until-spec-justified rule, false completion, spec betrayal with an explicit user>spec>tests>code authority order, the UNVERIFIABLE label). That third-party-agent auditing matters increasingly since David delegates to subagents and Workflow runs in Claude Code, and neither built-in verify nor code-review audits a report's claims or diffs test files for loosened assertions. It carries the best evidence of the collection (measured Haiku transfer 3.5/5 to 5/5 frauds caught). Two caveats keep it out of always-on: the trigger collision with adversarial-verify, and its dependencies on fable-method's failure-modes.md and domains/ fraud tables plus the repo's eval/ for suite mode (suite mode itself is repo-specific and skippable — David's research-methodology/governance-adoption-campaign own his eval discipline). Best disposition: install on-demand (explicit "/fable-judge"-style invocation when judging delegated or external agent work), and separately merge the named deltas — verdict triad, fraud frequency ordering, changed-test guilt rule, diff-against-pristine-copy for non-git work — into adversarial-verify via skill-authoring + research-methodology rather than duplicating the trigger surface.

verify.reasoning:
Refuted on all three checks after reading the candidate (scratchpad/fable-method/skills/fable-judge/SKILL.md), /home/user/claude-core-skills/.claude/skills/adversarial-verify/SKILL.md, /home/user/claude-core-skills/.claude/skills/architecture-contract/SKILL.md, the repo layout, and eval/results/round8-fable-judge-transfer.json.

(1) REDUNDANCY — the net-new claim ("neither built-in verify nor code-review nor adversarial-verify audits ANOTHER agent's report") is overstated. adversarial-verify already covers third-party artifacts explicitly ("when the user hands you something they made and asks you to check it... grade and try to refute their artifact"), already mandates exercise-over-inspect (re-run, two runs where cheap), already has a self-consistency check requiring claims to match artifacts mechanically (diff/wc/rerun), and already forbids inflated verdicts with candidate/open labels — functionally the VERIFIED/CAVEATS/REFUTED triad and UNVERIFIABLE label. Subagent output delivered by this session IS this session's deliverable, so adversarial-verify's primary mode fires on David's delegation case too. Built-in code-review owns diff bug hunting; built-in verify owns end-to-end exercise. The genuinely net-new residue is a short list of checklist deltas: the weakened-test guilty-until-spec-justified rule, the user>spec>tests>code authority order, frequency-ordered fraud taxonomy, pristine-copy diff for non-git work. Deltas, not a capability domain.

(2) ARCHITECTURE — three violations. Decision 3: the description has no NOT clause and its trigger phrases ("did that actually work?", "verify what it did", "judge this work") collide head-on with adversarial-verify and built-in verify — weak-point 2's double-load, on the library's highest-traffic trigger surface. Decision 5 / context-cost: the proposed "on-demand" placement doesn't exist as a mechanism — any installed skill's description sits in every session's context, so the collision cost is paid regardless of intent. Decision 2 corollary: the procedure hard-depends on sibling-repo facts (fable-method's references/failure-modes.md and references/domains/, which I verified live in the SIBLING skill directory, not in fable-judge; suite mode needs ../../eval/ or a clone of Sahir619/fable-method). Installed standalone as tiered, its fraud catalogue, domain adapters, and suite mode all dangle.

(3) SUBSTANCE — the body is concrete, not be-careful prose, but the evidence cited as "best of the collection" is a Haiku-only transfer eval (round8, 2026-07-06): bare Haiku already got verdict_correct=true and caught 3-4/5 frauds; the skill lifted fraud coverage to 5/5 but reran_verification stayed FALSE even skill-equipped — its central law didn't transfer in its own eval. By this library's own retirement standard (live-state-truth RETIRE-CONFIRMED because strong base models already do the behavior), a Haiku delta is not evidence of a Fable 5/Opus/Sonnet delta, and strong models plus adversarial-verify already carry most of this behavior. 

The original analyst's own best-disposition — merge the four named deltas into adversarial-verify via skill-authoring + research-methodology rather than install the skill — is correct, and it is the definition of NICE TO HAVE (a source to harvest), not HIGHLY RECOMMEND (a skill to install). Torn between tiers, the mandate says choose lower: NICE TO HAVE.

verdicts.name: anthropics/skills (docx, pdf, pptx, xlsx, webapp-testing, skill-creator, internal-comms, web-artifacts-builder)

inv.name: anthropics/skills (official Anthropic skill collection)
inv.what_it_is: The official Anthropic skills repository: 17 self-contained skills under skills/, plus the Agent Skills spec (spec/), a skill template (template/), and a .claude-plugin/marketplace.json that packages them as two installable Claude Code plugins ("document-skills" = docx/pdf/pptx/xlsx; "example-skills" = the rest). The four document skills are the actual production skills that power Claude's file-creation capabilities on claude.ai, shipped as source-available reference implementations with real Python tooling (OOXML unpack/edit/validate/repack against bundled ISO/IEC 29500 XSD schemas, tracked-changes acceptance, LibreOffice headless conversion, formula recalculation). The example skills cover webapp testing with Playwright, an eval-driven skill-creation workflow, internal-comms templates, a React/shadcn artifact bundler, MCP building, Claude API reference, and several design/art skills. It is the canonical, vendor-maintained reference for what a high-quality skill looks like.
inv.skills_inside: Shortlisted 8: docx — create/edit/analyze Word files via docx-js for new docs and unpack-XML-edit-repack for edits, with validate.py (XSD validation), accept_changes.py, comment.py, pandoc extraction (590-line SKILL.md + 61 files). pdf — pypdf/pdfplumber recipes plus 8 form-filling scripts (extract_form_structure, fill_fillable_fields, bounding-box checks, validation-image rendering) and forms.md/reference.md. pptx — markitdown extraction, pptxgenjs creation (dedicated pptxgenjs.md), XML editing workflow (editing.md), thumbnail/render validation via LibreOffice. xlsx — openpyxl workflows plus recalc.py using headless LibreOffice; encodes financial-modeling conventions (color coding, zero-formula-error requirement, number formats). webapp-testing — Playwright-based local webapp testing with with_server.py server-lifecycle manager and 3 example scripts. skill-creator — full skill authoring + eval loop: run_eval.py, run_loop.py, aggregate_benchmark.py, improve_description.py, package_skill.py, quick_validate.py, grader/analyzer/comparator agent prompts, HTML eval viewer. internal-comms — lightweight: 4 markdown format guides (3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, general) with a routing SKILL.md; prose only, no scripts. web-artifacts-builder — init-artifact.sh + bundle-artifact.sh to scaffold React 18/TS/Vite/Tailwind/shadcn projects and bundle to a single-file claude.ai artifact; ships a 40-component shadcn tarball. Also present (not shortlisted): algorithmic-art, brand-guidelines, canvas-design, claude-api, doc-coauthoring, frontend-design, mcp-builder, slack-gif-creator, theme-factory.
inv.quality: Very high — the reference standard. The document skills are production code (source-available versions of what runs behind Claude's file creation): substantive procedural SKILL.md bodies (232-590 lines) with decision tables, validation gates ("validate after every edit", "zero formula errors"), and dozens of working scripts including full ISO/IEC 29500 XSD schema validation for OOXML — none of this is knowledge a base model reliably executes unaided (raw OOXML editing without corrupting files, tracked-changes semantics, PDF AcroForm filling with bounding-box verification). skill-creator encodes a complete measurable eval methodology (benchmark variance analysis, description-trigger optimization) that goes well beyond model priors. internal-comms is the weakest of the eight — generic format templates a strong model could approximate. Actively maintained: last commit 2026-07-01 (12 days before evaluation date), with recent model-update commits (PR #1373). Minimal padding; prose is operational, not marketing.
inv.dependencies: No MCP servers or external APIs required for the document/testing skills; everything runs locally via Bash + Python/Node. Python: pypdf, pdfplumber, pdf2image, Pillow, openpyxl, defusedxml, markitdown[pptx], playwright (webapp-testing). Node: docx (docx-js), pptxgenjs; web-artifacts-builder needs Node 18+, npm, Vite/Parcel/Tailwind/shadcn (init script installs them — network access for npm/pip installs). CLI binaries: pandoc, LibreOffice (soffice; auto-configured for sandboxes via scripts/office/soffice.py), poppler-utils (pdftoppm). skill-creator's eval scripts additionally use the anthropic Python SDK (an API key) to run eval loops. internal-comms has zero dependencies.
inv.surfaces: Both, by design — but unevenly. The four document skills already run in production on claude.ai (code-execution sandbox) and install into Claude Code via the repo's own plugin marketplace (/plugin marketplace add anthropics/skills). internal-comms works anywhere (pure prose). webapp-testing and web-artifacts-builder are effectively Claude Code/local-environment only (need a local dev server, npm, Playwright browsers). skill-creator works best in Claude Code (spawns eval runs via the API/CLI). Everything requiring scripts assumes a Bash + Python + Node execution environment.
inv.license: Dual: example skills (webapp-testing, skill-creator, internal-comms, web-artifacts-builder, etc.) are Apache 2.0 per their per-skill LICENSE.txt; the document skills (docx, pdf, pptx, xlsx) are proprietary/source-available — © 2025 Anthropic PBC, use governed by your Anthropic services agreement (Consumer/Commercial ToS), explicitly "source-available, not open source" per the README. THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md covers bundled third-party components. No repo-root LICENSE file; licensing is per-skill.

verdicts.overlaps_with: claude.ai native document skills (docx/pdf/pptx/xlsx are already built into claude.ai); skill-authoring + research-methodology + diagnostics-and-tooling (skill-creator's authoring guidance and eval methodology overlap, though none of the three ship an automated eval runner); brand-standard (covers internal-comms' territory for David's actual comms); Claude Code built-ins verify/run (partial overlap with webapp-testing); claude.ai artifacts + artifact-design built-in (cover most of web-artifacts-builder)
verdicts.surfaces: Claude Code is where the value is: docx/pdf/pptx/xlsx install cleanly via the repo's own plugin marketplace (/plugin marketplace add anthropics/skills → document-skills plugin) and need only local Bash+Python+Node+LibreOffice/pandoc. On claude.ai the document skills are redundant (natively shipped). webapp-testing, skill-creator, and web-artifacts-builder are Claude Code/local-only (Playwright browsers, npm, API-key eval runs); skill-creator's eval scripts additionally need the anthropic SDK and an API key.
verdicts.license: Dual, per-skill: docx/pdf/pptx/xlsx are proprietary/source-available (© 2025 Anthropic PBC, governed by your Anthropic services agreement — fine for David's personal use, verified in pdf/LICENSE.txt); example skills (webapp-testing, skill-creator, internal-comms, web-artifacts-builder) are Apache 2.0 (verified per-skill LICENSE.txt). No repo-root LICENSE.
verdicts.rationale: The four document skills are net-new on exactly one of David's two equal surfaces: Claude Code has no built-in docx/pptx/xlsx/pdf capability, and these are the production-grade workflows (unpack-XML-edit-repack with XSD validation, tracked-changes acceptance, AcroForm filling with bounding-box checks, LibreOffice recalc) that a base model cannot execute without corrupting OOXML files — high-frequency leverage for David's documents/office and personal-ops domains (e.g. brand-standard resumes rendered to real .docx). They stop short of MUST ADD only because half the footprint is redundant (claude.ai ships them natively) and four heavy skills fail the always-on context bar, so the right move is installing the vendor-maintained document-skills plugin in Claude Code (on-demand/plugin, zero maintenance) and never uploading them to claude.ai. Second standout: skill-creator's eval harness (run_eval.py, run_loop.py, aggregate_benchmark.py, improve_description.py) fills the precise tooling gap David's own library names — research-methodology and governance-adoption-campaign Phase 3 want cheap repeated trigger evals but ship no runner; harvest the scripts as tooling while keeping skill-authoring as the style authority, since the two houses' authoring conventions differ. webapp-testing is a modest add over the built-in verify/run (with_server.py lifecycle management is real but niche). Skip internal-comms (generic templates; brand-standard + base model cover it) and web-artifacts-builder (claude.ai artifacts + artifact-design built-in cover the need for a non-frontend-heavy user).

verify.reasoning: REDUNDANCY: Net-new claim verified against actual files. Claude Code ships no Office-document capability (confirmed by this session's built-in skill list); the candidate docx/pdf/pptx/xlsx skills carry executable tooling a base model cannot reproduce — unpack/pack/validate pipeline with 40+ XSD schemas, accept_changes.py (LibreOffice tracked-changes acceptance), comment.py plumbing across five XML parts, pdf form-filling with bounding-box checks, xlsx recalc.py. Applying the live-state-truth retirement standard, these survive: the docx SKILL.md's 590 lines encode non-obvious, corruption-preventing facts (WidthType.PERCENTAGE breaks Google Docs, ShadingType.CLEAR vs SOLID, w:del inside w:pPr/w:rPr for paragraph-mark deletion) that measurement would not retire. The claude.ai-side redundancy is real but already priced into the record's placement (Claude Code plugin only, never upload to claude.ai) and its denial of MUST ADD. The skill-creator eval-harness gap claim is confirmed: the core library ships only diagnostics-and-tooling/scripts/lint_skill.sh (a structural linter), while skill-creator ships run_eval.py, run_loop.py, aggregate_benchmark.py, improve_description.py plus grader/comparator/analyzer agents and an eval viewer. One overstatement found: the library's results/2026-07-11/ dirs contain bespoke one-off runners (run_arm.py, runner.py, grade.py) from the Phase 2 campaign, so the gap is 'no reusable maintained harness' rather than 'no runner ever' — this weakens but does not overturn the claim. ARCHITECTURE: Read architecture-contract/SKILL.md in full. On-demand plugin placement touches no invariant: it never enters this repo's .claude/skills tree (invariant 8 placement law intact; candidate name: fields match directories anyway), respects Decision 5's context-footprint discipline via on-demand loading, and the record's 'skill-authoring remains style authority, harvest skill-creator scripts as tooling' correctly applies Decision 2's instance-wins precedence. The docx description satisfies Decision 3's WHEN/quoted-triggers/NOT contract. SUBSTANCE: docx (590 lines), pdf (314 lines + 8 scripts), xlsx (291 lines + recalc), skill-creator (485 lines + 9 scripts) are tooling, not be-careful prose. The three weak members — webapp-testing (95 lines of Playwright patterns a strong base model has natively), internal-comms (32 lines + generic templates), web-artifacts-builder (73 lines; covered by claude.ai artifacts + artifact-design built-in) — would flunk the retirement standard, but the original record already excluded all three from its recommendation, so they do not carry the tier. The tier's load-bearing core — production-grade document tooling net-new on Claude Code serving a domain evidenced by the library's own brand-standard skill (resumes, letters, styled PDFs/documents in David's name), plus a reusable eval harness for a gap the library names in governance-adoption-campaign — survived all refutation attempts. Not torn between tiers: HIGHLY RECOMMEND at on-demand/plugin placement stands, and MUST ADD remains correctly denied given the claude.ai redundancy and four-skill context weight.

verdicts.name: sanjay3290/ai-skills (Google Workspace suite, postgres/mysql/mssql, notebooklm, deep-research, elevenlabs, imagen, jules, outline, azure-devops)

inv.name: sanjay3290/ai-skills
inv.what_it_is: A 24-skill collection (Claude Code plugin marketplace manifest v1.2.0, also installable via `npx skills` into 40+ agents) where each skill is a thin SKILL.md wrapper around substantial runnable Python CLI scripts (~19,500 lines total). Coverage spans three areas: personal-ops (7 Google Workspace skills with standalone OAuth + keyring token storage, Telegram/WhatsApp messaging, Atlassian, Azure DevOps, Outline wiki), data access (read-only postgres/mysql/mssql query executors with multi-connection config), and AI delegation (Gemini Deep Research, NotebookLM via Playwright automation, Jules, Manus, Grok Build, ElevenLabs/Google TTS narration, Imagen). The Google Workspace skills piggyback on the official Gemini CLI workspace extension's OAuth infrastructure — they hardcode its client ID and route token refresh through the third-party cloud function at google-workspace-extension.geminicli.com, and require a Google Workspace account (personal Gmail unsupported). One caveat on the shortlist rationale: the "linked eight-plus times across awesome lists" signal is partly self-generated — a hidden HTML comment in the README (lines 435-460) tracks the author's forks of four awesome-list repos specifically for PR self-submissions.
inv.skills_inside: 24 skills under skills/: postgres — read-only PostgreSQL with real defense-in-depth (server-side readonly=True session, prefix validation, single-statement check, 30s timeout, 10k-row cap, credential-sanitized errors); mysql — same pattern with session-level write protection; mssql — same with query-validation-only security; gmail — search/read/send/labels via OAuth (gmail.modify scope); google-calendar — events, scheduling, free-time lookup; google-docs — create/read/edit docs; google-sheets — read spreadsheets/ranges; google-slides — read presentations (read-only); google-drive — search/list/download; google-chat — spaces, messages, DMs; notebooklm — Playwright headless-Chrome automation for querying/managing notebooks, with persistent profiles, batch queries, source sync/dedupe, and a pytest test suite (the only skill with tests); deep-research — Gemini Deep Research Agent wrapper ($2-5/query, 2-10 min, streaming); elevenlabs — TTS narration + two-host podcast from PDF/DOCX/MD; google-tts — same via Google Cloud TTS; imagen — Gemini image generation; jules — delegate coding tasks to Google Jules (CLI or API-key paths); manus — delegate research/reports to Manus API; grok-build — orchestrate headless Grok CLI coding with diff review; outline — Outline wiki CRUD; atlassian — Jira + Confluence (OAuth 2.1 or API token, 835-line jira.py + 742-line confluence.py); azure-devops — 15 script modules claiming 99 tools across 13 domains (work items, repos, PRs, pipelines, wikis, test plans, security, approvals); apple-container — Apple-silicon container CLI reference (docs-only, no scripts); telegram — bot messaging, files, ask-and-wait approvals, hook integration; whatsapp — messaging via 753-line wa.py.
inv.quality: High-substance, low-padding. SKILL.md files are lean (54-477 lines) and procedural — setup, exact commands, intent-to-database matching tables, troubleshooting tables, exit codes — while the real value lives in ~19.5k lines of working Python: full OAuth flows (PKCE-style local callback server, keyring storage, refresh handling), a 1,564-line NotebookLM browser-automation manager with retries and artifact capture, and a genuinely careful postgres executor whose primary protection is a server-side readonly session rather than mere prompt-level string checks. NotebookLM ships pytest tests; most skills have per-skill READMEs. Maintenance is active: last commit 2026-07-09 (4 days before evaluation date), versioned plugin manifest, recently added marketplace install path. What it encodes beyond a base model is not knowledge but pre-built plumbing: working OAuth against Google's cloud function, keyring token storage, Playwright session persistence — things a model could write but not ship pre-wired and debugged. Weaknesses: the mssql skill relies on query validation alone (no server-side readonly enforcement like postgres); the client-side is_read_only check is a simple prefix whitelist; single-author project with no CI visible; and the awesome-list ubiquity that drove shortlisting is partly self-promotion per the README's hidden comment.
inv.dependencies: Heavy and per-skill: Python 3.8+ with pip packages (psycopg2-binary, mysql-connector-python, pymssql, keyring [required by 9 auth.py scripts], httpx, playwright>=1.49 + chromium install for NotebookLM, PyPDF2/python-docx for TTS extraction); system binaries: ffmpeg (both TTS skills), npm CLIs @google/jules and grok, Apple's container CLI (macOS-only skill); API keys/accounts: GEMINI_API_KEY (imagen, deep-research), ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, GOOGLE_TTS_API_KEY, MANUS_API_KEY, OUTLINE_API_KEY, Azure DevOps PAT/OAuth, Atlassian OAuth/token, Telegram bot token; Google Workspace skills need a Workspace (not personal) Google account, a browser for first-time OAuth, OS keyring (Keychain/Credential Locker/Secret Service), and trust in the third-party token-refresh cloud function at google-workspace-extension.geminicli.com with a hardcoded client ID; databases need a connections.json with plaintext credentials (chmod 600 advised). No MCP servers required (Atlassian optionally uses one for OAuth). Network access required by nearly everything.
inv.surfaces: Claude Code only (plus other CLI agents: Gemini CLI, Cursor, Codex, Goose). Every skill depends on locally executed Python scripts, OS keyring access, browser-launching OAuth flows, persistent local config/profile files, and outbound network — none of which claude.ai's skill sandbox provides. The repo itself targets the multi-agent Agent Skills Standard and ships a Claude Code plugin marketplace manifest; nothing here is claude.ai-compatible.
